assertions.expectation: Configures a recurring maintenance window for weekends only
assertions.reason: Uses --maintenance-window-recurrence "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA" to schedule maintenance only on Saturdays

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command with --maintenance-window-start/end and --maintenance-window-recurrence for weekly weekend schedule
assertions.reason: Includes --maintenance-window-start, --maintenance-window-duration, and --maintenance-window-recurrence parameters with Saturday-only scheduling

assertions.expectation: Sets up maintenance exclusions for quarterly code freezes (up to 30 days each)
assertions.reason: Provides example command for Q1 2025 code freeze with 30-day duration using --add-maintenance-exclusion-* parameters

assertions.expectation: Sets up a maintenance exclusion for the November audit period
assertions.reason: Provides specific command for November audit exclusion from 2024-11-01 to 2024-12-01

assertions.expectation: Notes the 30-day maximum for maintenance exclusions and how to handle longer freezes
assertions.reason: Does not mention the 30-day maximum limit for maintenance exclusions or provide guidance for handling longer freeze periods

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows — only auto-upgrades respect them
assertions.reason: Explicitly states that manual upgrades bypass exclusions and provides example commands for manual upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends combining maintenance windows with Stable or Extended channel for maximum upgrade control
assertions.reason: Recommends Extended channel and explains how it provides maximum control with 24-month support

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for each exclusion (--add-maintenance-exclusion-name/start-time/end-time)
assertions.reason: Includes complete gcloud commands with --add-maintenance-exclusion-name, --add-maintenance-exclusion-start-time, and --add-maintenance-exclusion-end-time parameters

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during long exclusion periods
assertions.reason: Mentions the risk of snowflaking and explains that persistent exclusions track EoS to prevent indefinite freezing
